scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to specialized commercial courts provides direct legal and material advantages for trade disputes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad legal right spanning all commercial maritime loans and shipments.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Persistence lasts as long as one maintains the occupation of shipowner or merchant.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation as a shipowner is a durable social identity but can be left.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Demosthenes describes a law in force in his own time in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Directly quotes and cites the law as an established statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a shipowner does not inherently define access to specialized legal procedures.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Specialized legal venues facilitate the recovery of debt and material assets.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad category of maritime trade and debt litigation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies to the duration of the individual's career in commerce.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Merchant status is an acquired professional identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Relates to the contemporary Athenian legal system.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the law 'ordains' the specific procedure as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Court admissibility is a legal privilege, not a definition of being a merchant.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Freedom from imprisonment is a fundamental bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad rule affecting the personal liberty of non-citizens during litigation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The detention is bounded to the duration of a single trial procedure.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Non-resident/metic status is a legal birth-status and hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the current procedure under the Athenian Polemarch.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes standard legal procedure as a reporting factual matter.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a non-resident does not definitionally include a lack of bail.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A dowry consists of tangible wealth and currency (40 minae).
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis regarding family wealth and female maintenance.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Dowry typically lasts for the duration of a marriage.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender and lineage (daughterhood) are biologically/birth fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary Athenian marital and inheritance customs.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Records the transfer of the portion as an established social fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Womanhood does not inherently imply possessing a 40-minae portion.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Marital status determines one's domicile, legal protector, and economic support.
scores.generality_reasoning: Affects the fundamental life choice of marriage partner.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Impacts the long-term domestic life of the woman until the next marital change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group is defined by gender (women/daughters).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Observed practice in the author's current society.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Narrates a specific instance illustrating broader patriarchal rights.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Lack of freedom in marriage is not the definition of being a woman.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Relates to the ownership and possession of tangible mortgaged property.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad legal protection for creditors across all mortgage types.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as the mortgage exists or until the debt is settled.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Creditor status is based on wealth and specific financial activity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Direct quote of an Athenian law then in force.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author cites the 'law which expressly ordains' as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A 'right to property' for a 'creditor' regarding a 'mortgage' is borderline definitional of being a mortgagee.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical jewelry and bowls are tangible material goods.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific right to moveables and personal loans.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Possession of personal items is generally lifelong or long-term.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Based on gender (wife/woman).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary Athenian social reality.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports evidence of personal property as a factual background to the case.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman doesn't definitionally include owning specific jewelry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal signaling on property directly impacts property value and ownership claims.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific procedural mechanism (mortgage pillars).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Ends once the debt is paid or the property title is transferred.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status as a property owner or claimant can change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Standard Athenian legal practice of the time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the direction for pillars to be set up as per established practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Property ownership doesn't inherently include the right to set up horoi.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Control over property inventory has direct tax and litigation consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Detailed procedural rule for a specific type of trial (antidosis).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Limited to the duration of the inventory submission phase.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealthy status is durable but not immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites current Athenian statutes regarding the antidosis.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States 'the law prescribes' as a factual procedural requirement.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Defining wealth does not involve a 3-day deadline for paperwork.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Direct physical control over buildings and grain stores.
scores.generality_reasoning: Restricted to the specific context of property exchange proceedings.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Temporary protection during the litigation period.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status dependent on being involved in a wealthy property dispute.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to active Athenian law concerning exchange.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Refers to what 'the law permitted me' as a matter of fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Property ownership does not definitionally include 'sealing' rights during lawsuits.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Involves heavy financial obligations (advancing the entire amount of money).
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad system of public financing and state service.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Membership in the Three Hundred is a long-term status based on net worth.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Based on wealth brackets, which are changeable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the current tax/liturgy structure of Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim describes the operation of the law and tax groups as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being rich does not inherently mean being part of the 'Three Hundred'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Direct exemption from significant public taxes/liturgies.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the sector of silver mining investment.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong/structural exemption for any assets held in mines.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation/investment choice is acquired.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Author cites existing Athenian law in his own time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Plain citation of the law 'which declares'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Mining doesn't definitionally entail tax exemption.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to own the capital of the mother's marriage portion.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad rule of inheritance and family property law.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Once acquired, the dowry remains part of the son's estate.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Biological and birth-status (sonhood/age).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses Athenian family law in the present tense.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States 'the laws make him the owner' as a pure legal fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a son does not definitionally include owning a mother's dowry.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Involves the massive expense of funding a trireme.
scores.generality_reasoning: A major foundational axis of Athenian military/financial participation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Obligation recurs as long as one remains in the top wealth bracket.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is durable but not immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Relates to the author's own recent public service.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Report of actual service performed under the law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealth does not definitionally mean serving as a trierarch.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from physical torture (corporal punishment).
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad rule governing slave testimony and bodily safety.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Permanent condition of legal exposure for slaves in court cases.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Legal birth-status; hard to leave (requires manumission).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes active Athenian courtroom procedural norms.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes legal necessity ('it is necessary') as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slavery does not definitionally include 'torture via challenge'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Public services (liturgies) involve substantial material/financial transfers.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad axis of civic and fiscal participation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong obligation following naturalization.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Naturalized status is a permanent legal category once attained.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: States the current expectations for new citizens in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mixes 'what the laws require' with a normative claim ('ought to show').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Naturalization does not definitionally include 'gratitude' or extra taxes.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Free speech in assemblies has direct political and legal effect.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis of Athenian democratic life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong right attached to citizenship status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is legal birth-status, very hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Fundamental contemporary Athenian norm.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cited as a standard component of 'citizenship' as a known fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship is not definitionally 'free speech' in every society.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The fine (one-sixth of amount) is a direct financial loss.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific procedural penalty in specific trial types.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A one-time momentary penalty for a single failed case.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Episodic rolebound status as a 'litigant'.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites an active rule of Athenian private law litigation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the fine amount and condition as a matter of fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a litigant doesn't definitionally mean being fined 1/6th.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Total ownership and control over all wealth and earnings.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational axis of the slave system and economic rights.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds throughout the duration of the state of enslavement.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a hard legal/social birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Standard social/legal rule of the author's present.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Applies a known legal principle to frame the case.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being property (a slave) definitionally implies one's earnings belong to the owner.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to claim an estate affects the inheritance of significant property.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to probate and estate law procedures.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Limited to the period when a specific estate is open for claim.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Non-citizen (implicit group) is a hard legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes Athenian inheritance laws in force.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Directly quotes 'the laws ordain' as a factual statement.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Non-citizenship does not definitionally forbid estate claiming.
